result.markdown:
# Spaghetti Plots



## Spaghetti / profile plot

`hv_spaghetti()` draws one trajectory line per subject over time, with optional
stratification by a grouping variable. `plot()` accepts `add_smooth = TRUE` for
an optional LOESS overlay and returns a bare ggplot. `sample_spaghetti_data()`
generates 150 patients with up to 6 observations each, stratified by a named
proportion vector.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
dta_sp <- sample_spaghetti_data(
  n_patients = 150,
  max_obs    = 6,
  groups     = c(Female = 0.45, Male = 0.55),
  seed       = 42L
)
head(dta_sp)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  id time value  group
1  1 0.44 22.12 Female
2  1 0.67 27.20 Female
3  1 0.91 29.24 Female
4  1 1.29 18.95 Female
5  1 1.96 24.00 Female
6  2 2.04 25.16 Female
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Build S3 objects for reuse below
sp     <- hv_spaghetti(dta_sp)
sp_col <- hv_spaghetti(dta_sp, colour_col = "group")
```
:::


### Bare plot

The bare `plot(sp)` panel draws one thin trajectory per patient over time with
no colour, axis limits, or theme.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
p_sp <- plot(sp)
p_sp
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](spaghetti_files/figure-html/spaghetti_bare-1.png){width=1050}
:::
:::


### Unstratified

Set axis limits and breaks with the usual scale layers, then add a theme.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(sp) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +
  labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](spaghetti_files/figure-html/spaghetti_unstrat_full-1.png){width=1050}
:::
:::


### Stratified by group

Build the object with `colour_col = "group"` so each subject's line is coloured
by its stratum, then assign colours with `scale_colour_manual()`.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(sp_col) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +
  labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](spaghetti_files/figure-html/spaghetti_sex_strat-1.png){width=1050}
:::
:::


### With LOESS smooth overlay

Pass `add_smooth = TRUE` to `plot()` to add a LOESS trend line per group over
the individual trajectories.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(sp_col, add_smooth = TRUE) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(Female = "firebrick", Male = "steelblue"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 5, 1)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 80, 20)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 80)) +
  labs(x = "Years", y = "AV Mean Gradient (mmHg)") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](spaghetti_files/figure-html/spaghetti_smooth-1.png){width=1050}
:::
:::


## Temporal trend plot

`hv_trends()` accepts patient-level data and computes annual summaries (mean or
median) internally. `plot()` returns a bare ggplot; add scales, labels, and a
theme. `sample_trends_data()` generates patient-level data that `hv_trends()`
aggregates by year; the `year_range` and `groups` arguments mirror the study
period and group structure.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
dta_tr <- sample_trends_data(
  n          = 600,
  year_range = c(1968L, 2000L),
  groups     = c("Group I", "Group II", "Group III", "Group IV")
)
head(dta_tr)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
  year value    group
1 1975 68.94  Group I
2 1973 58.54  Group I
3 1977 42.45  Group I
4 1994 42.06  Group I
5 1987 35.46 Group II
6 1992 27.59 Group IV
```


:::
:::


### Single variable: cases/year

Subset to a single group and fit with `group_col = NULL` for a single annual
series, then set axis limits and breaks to match the study window.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
one_grp <- dta_tr[dta_tr$group == "Group I", ]
tr1 <- hv_trends(one_grp, group_col = NULL)

plot(tr1) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1968, 2000), breaks = seq(1968, 2000, 4)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 10),      breaks = seq(0, 10, 2)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Cases/year") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](spaghetti_files/figure-html/trends_n_year-1.png){width=1050}
:::
:::


### Multiple groups

When `group_col` is set, the constructor computes per-group annual means and
`plot()` draws one line per group. `scale_colour_brewer()` and
`scale_shape_manual()` give each group a distinct colour and marker, keeping
the figure readable in greyscale.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
tr <- hv_trends(dta_tr)
plot(tr) +
  scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Group") +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("Group I" = 15L, "Group II" = 19L,
               "Group III" = 17L, "Group IV" = 18L),
    name = "Group"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1968, 2000), breaks = seq(1968, 2000, 4)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Outcome (%)") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](spaghetti_files/figure-html/trends_multi_brewer-1.png){width=1050}
:::
:::


### With confidence ribbon

Pass `se = TRUE` to `plot()` to add a `geom_ribbon()` around the mean line; the
`alpha` argument controls ribbon opacity.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(tr1, se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1968, 2000), breaks = seq(1968, 2000, 4)) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Cases/year") +
  theme_hv_manuscript()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](spaghetti_files/figure-html/trends_ribbon-1.png){width=1050}
:::
:::
